event: 30 years old
African American
Woman
Presented to the emergency department
Complaining of shortness of breath on exertion
Complaining of chest pain with deep inspiration
No indication of recent infections
Physical examination did not yield any abnormalities
VQ scan indicated high probability for pulmonary emboli in her right lobe
Chest X-ray showed possible development of small bilateral pleural effusions
Multiple nodular opacities scattered throughout the lungs were noted
CT scan of abdomen showed moderate alveolar consolidation in the right lower lobe
EKG was normal
2D echocardiogram was normal
No indication of endocarditis
Admitted for treatment of acute pulmonary embolism
Heparin drip initiated
Developed high fevers during hospital stay
Associated tachycardia
Blood cultures positive for Gram positive cocci in pairs and clusters
Organism identified as Staphylococcus caprae
Started on vancomycin
Antibiotics changed to Kefzol 2 gm IV every 8 hours
Fever resolved
Significant clinical improvement
Urinalysis indicated presence of E. coli
Three days of Ciprofloxacin administered for urinary tract infection
No recent visit to a farm
No contact with goats
No history of transplant
No history of internal fixation for orthopedic prosthesis
No history of foreign body implants
Repeat echocardiogram did not reveal evidence of endocarditis
